plants.name: Monstera

watering.frequencyNote: Allow soil to dry out between waterings
watering.amount: Water thoroughly until it drains freely
watering.seasonalNote: Growth slows, so the soil stays wet longer

feeding.winterNote: Plant is dormant and won't use nutrients

light.placement: Near a north or east-facing window
light.placementNote: A few feet back from a south-facing window also works

environment.temperatureNote: Keep away from cold draughts and radiators
environment.humidityNote: Mist leaves or use a pebble tray to boost humidity

tips: Wipe leaves with a damp cloth occasionally to remove dust and help the plant absorb light
Repot every 1–2 years in spring when roots start coming through the drainage holes
The aerial roots can be tucked into the soil or trained up a moss pole for support
Yellow leaves usually mean overwatering — check the soil before watering again

plants.name: Snake Plant

watering.frequencyNote: One of the most drought-tolerant houseplants
watering.amount: Water moderately, letting the soil dry out completely first
watering.seasonal: Reduce to once a month or less in winter
watering.seasonalNote: Overwatering in cold months is the most common cause of death

feeding.fertiliser: General-purpose liquid fertiliser at half strength
feeding.winterNote: Plant is near dormant in low light and cold

light.placement: Tolerates most spots in the home
light.placementNote: Will grow faster in brighter light but survives in dim corners
light.avoidNote: Can bleach or scorch the leaves

environment.temperatureNote: Avoid temperatures below 10°C — cold drafts will damage it
environment.humidityNote: Does not need extra humidity — very adaptable

tips: One of the best plants for beginners — very hard to kill
Always err on the side of underwatering rather than overwatering
Wipe dust from the leaves occasionally to keep them looking their best
Can be propagated easily by dividing at the root or taking leaf cuttings

plants.name: Fiddle Leaf Fig

watering.frequencyNote: Water when the top 5cm of soil is dry
watering.amount: Water thoroughly, then allow to drain completely
watering.seasonalNote: Less active growth means less water needed

feeding.winterNote: Growth stops and excess nutrients can damage roots

light.placement: Close to a south or west-facing window
light.placementNote: Needs more light than most houseplants to thrive
light.avoid: Low light and direct midday sun
light.avoidNote: Low light causes leggy growth; direct sun scorches leaves

environment.temperatureNote: Hates cold drafts and sudden temperature changes — do not move it often
environment.humidityNote: Mist regularly or use a humidifier in dry rooms

tips: Consistency is key — it hates being moved and will drop leaves in protest
Rotate the pot a quarter turn each month for even, upright growth
Brown spots on leaves usually indicate root rot from overwatering
Dust the large leaves regularly so they can photosynthesize efficiently

plants.name: Cactus

watering.frequencyNote: Allow soil to dry out completely between waterings
watering.seasonal: Once a month or less in winter
watering.seasonalNote: Most cacti are dormant in winter and need very little water

feeding.frequencyNote: During the growing season only (spring and summer)
feeding.winterNote: Dormant period — feeding can cause weak, leggy growth

light.ideal: Full sun or very bright indirect light
light.placementNote: The more direct sun the better for most species
light.avoidNote: Will become etiolated (stretched and pale) without enough light

environment.temperatureNote: Many species tolerate cold winters down to 5–10°C if kept dry
environment.humidityNote: Thrives in dry air — avoid humid bathrooms or kitchens

tips: Use a gritty, free-draining cactus compost to prevent root rot
Terracotta pots are ideal as they allow moisture to evaporate through the sides
Handle with thick gloves or folded newspaper to avoid spines
A cool, dry winter rest period often encourages flowering in spring

plants.name: Peace Lily

watering.frequencyNote: It will visibly droop when thirsty — a reliable signal
watering.amount: Water thoroughly until it drains from the bottom
watering.seasonalNote: Less light means slower water uptake

feeding.winterNote: Plant is resting and doesn't need extra nutrients

light.placement: Away from windows or in a shaded spot
light.placementNote: One of the few flowering plants that thrives in low light
light.avoidNote: Will scorch and yellow the leaves quickly

environment.temperatureNote: Avoid anything below 13°C and cold window drafts
environment.humidityNote: Mist frequently or keep near a humidifier — loves moisture

tips: Toxic to cats and dogs — keep out of reach of pets
Yellow leaves usually mean too much direct light or overwatering
Flowers best when slightly root-bound, so don't rush to repot
Wipe the broad leaves occasionally to keep them dust-free

plants.name: Pothos

watering.frequencyNote: Allow the top half of the soil to dry out between waterings
watering.amount: Water thoroughly, then let it drain
watering.seasonalNote: Slower growth means slower water uptake

feeding.winterNote: Growth slows significantly in low winter light

light.placementNote: Variegated varieties need more light to keep their pattern

environment.temperatureNote: Avoid cold drafts and temperatures below 10°C

tips: One of the easiest houseplants to propagate — just snip a node and place in water
Train trailing vines up a moss pole or let them cascade from a shelf
Toxic to pets — keep away from cats and dogs
Yellowing leaves are usually a sign of overwatering

plants.name: Parlour Palm

watering.frequencyNote: Keep soil lightly moist — not soggy, not bone dry
watering.amount: Water moderately and allow the top inch to dry before watering again
watering.seasonalNote: Less light and cooler temps slow growth and water needs

feeding.fertiliser: Liquid palm fertiliser or general houseplant feed
feeding.winterNote: Dormant — feeding can cause salt build-up in the soil

light.placement: Away from direct sun, near a bright room
light.avoidNote: Fronds will scorch and turn yellow

environment.temperatureNote: Avoid cold drafts — keep away from exterior doors in winter
environment.humidityNote: Mist occasionally or group with other plants to raise humidity

tips: One of the most tolerant palms for indoor conditions
Brown leaf tips are usually caused by dry air or fluoride in tap water — try filtered water
Non-toxic to cats and dogs — a safe choice for pet owners
Slow growing — don't expect rapid height gains

plants.name: Aloe Vera

watering.frequencyNote: Allow the soil to dry out completely between waterings
watering.amount: Water deeply, then leave until fully dry again
watering.seasonalNote: Very little water needed when growth has stopped

feeding.winterNote: Dormant — feeding is unnecessary and can cause harm

light.placementNote: Needs at least 6 hours of light a day to stay compact and healthy
light.avoidNote: Will become floppy and pale without enough light

environment.temperatureNote: Cannot tolerate frost — bring indoors if kept outside in summer
environment.humidityNote: Prefers dry air — avoid kitchens and bathrooms

tips: Use a cactus or succulent compost mixed with perlite for best drainage
The gel inside the leaves can be applied directly to minor burns and skin irritation
Toxic if ingested by pets — keep out of reach of cats and dogs
Offsets (pups) grow at the base and can be separated to propagate new plants
